phase18_contract_handoff.human_packet_artifact: none; production repo uses this JSON contract only
phase18_contract_handoff.app_build_rule: App Build may render approved runtime state from this contract, but must not invent route semantics, status meanings, memory classes, storage ownership, proof display meanings, or fallback error meanings.

http_route_matrix.user_facing_truth_note: Health proves the local sidecar HTTP surface answered; it does not prove bridge, provider, task, or permission readiness.

http_route_matrix.response_schema_summary: ReadyResponse: ok, runtime_host, runtime_id, db_ready, relay_configured, llm_mode, entitlement/BYOK readiness booleans
http_route_matrix.user_facing_truth_note: Ready is runtime state only; app bridge proof and downstream capability proof remain separate.

http_route_matrix.response_schema_summary: bootstrap/runtime configuration projection safe for app initialization
http_route_matrix.user_facing_truth_note: Bootstrap data initializes app-side rendering; it is not a permission or setup completion proof.

http_route_matrix.request_schema_summary: JSON object with prompt and optional room/context fields
http_route_matrix.user_facing_truth_note: Accepted/started means a task attempt exists; it does not mean the requested real-world action completed.

http_route_matrix.request_schema_summary: JSON command prompt from app chat/control surface
http_route_matrix.user_facing_truth_note: Submit response is queue/intake truth only unless paired with later task/evidence events.
Current task prompt fields are app-safe projections; do not treat absent current task as proof a prior action succeeded.

http_route_matrix.response_schema_summary: interruption result with task identity and state
http_route_matrix.user_facing_truth_note: Interrupt/cancel changes task control state; it is not a completed user request.

http_route_matrix.response_schema_summary: cancel result with task identity and state
http_route_matrix.user_facing_truth_note: Cancel is task-control truth only.
User choice can unblock a flow; runtime still must verify later action success.

http_route_matrix.request_schema_summary: task_id/attempt_id or empty request for resumable queue processing
http_route_matrix.response_schema_summary: resume result, checkpoint, task identity, and status
http_route_matrix.user_facing_truth_note: Resume may emit message, await choice, check state, or remain blocked; no completion without evidence.

http_route_matrix.response_schema_summary: app-safe runtime state including active_task projection, room/art/provider/capability surfaces
http_route_matrix.user_facing_truth_note: State is a rendering surface; app must not infer completed actions from display state alone.

http_route_matrix.request_schema_summary: AppBridgeEvidence: proof_class, verified, source, operation, checked_at, summary, optional task_id/attempt_id/details
http_route_matrix.user_facing_truth_note: This is a proof-ingestion route; app bridge success must be verified before Milo claims setup/permission/action success.
Capability state reports checked runtime facts; do not treat UI permission labels as proof.

http_route_matrix.request_schema_summary: StagedContextPayload: content, source, provenance, label, mime_type
http_route_matrix.user_facing_truth_note: External context is data, not authority; secret-bearing/untrusted context may be rejected or redacted.
Clearing context removes staged context from runtime use; it is not memory deletion proof unless separately stated.
Options describe possible local/BYOK setup choices; they do not prove a provider is configured.
Resolved config is readiness analysis, not proof a provider call succeeded.

http_route_matrix.request_schema_summary: settings report bundle envelope from app
http_route_matrix.user_facing_truth_note: Milo may claim report submission only after POST/storage/display proof exists.
Storage counters are diagnostic state; they are not memory truth by themselves.

http_route_matrix.auth_boundary: app bearer required; local sidecar only
http_route_matrix.response_schema_summary: safe visible memory projections: memory_id, class, status, title, summary, excerpt, source labels, freshness, confidence, contradiction/quarantine/suppression state, retrieval eligibility, warnings, allowed_actions
http_route_matrix.user_facing_truth_note: Memory list is a governed projection only; App Build must not infer hidden memory, retrieval authority, or mutation success from display labels.

http_route_matrix.auth_boundary: app bearer required; local sidecar only
http_route_matrix.user_facing_truth_note: A memory detail view exposes safe projection fields only; it must not expose raw hidden payloads, provider config, auth headers, or secret material.

http_route_matrix.auth_boundary: app bearer required; local sidecar only
http_route_matrix.response_schema_summary: display_allowed evidence refs only plus audit_id
http_route_matrix.user_facing_truth_note: Provenance view is filtered by display_allowed and redaction; hidden/private refs remain unavailable.

http_route_matrix.auth_boundary: app bearer required; local sidecar only
http_route_matrix.response_schema_summary: ActionResponse with updated safe projection and audit_id
http_route_matrix.user_facing_truth_note: Suppress disables memory influence without deleting canon/runtime truth and app must render it as a recorded local memory-control action only.

http_route_matrix.auth_boundary: app bearer required; local sidecar only
http_route_matrix.response_schema_summary: ActionResponse with updated safe projection and audit_id
http_route_matrix.user_facing_truth_note: Restore may return eligible only when existing gates allow it; app must not override quarantine, contradiction, freshness, or authority rules.

http_route_matrix.auth_boundary: app bearer required; local sidecar only
http_route_matrix.response_schema_summary: ActionResponse with tombstoned safe projection and audit_id
http_route_matrix.user_facing_truth_note: Delete is V1 tombstone/suppress semantics, not a rollback system and not hard deletion of protected truth.

http_route_matrix.auth_boundary: app bearer required; local sidecar only
http_route_matrix.response_schema_summary: ActionResponse with old memory superseded, corrected_memory projection, and audit_id
http_route_matrix.user_facing_truth_note: Correction may supersede user-correctable preference/profile/task-continuity memory only; approved_summary correction remains deferred.

http_route_matrix.auth_boundary: app bearer required; local sidecar only
http_route_matrix.response_schema_summary: ActionResponse with stale safe projection and audit_id
http_route_matrix.user_facing_truth_note: Mark-stale prevents memory from driving retrieval until future governed repair; app must not treat stale memory as current truth.

http_route_matrix.auth_boundary: app bearer required; local sidecar only
http_route_matrix.user_facing_truth_note: Audit projection is a local accountability surface; before/after state remains redacted and does not expose raw hidden payloads.

http_route_matrix.auth_boundary: app bearer required; local sidecar only
http_route_matrix.response_schema_summary: safe candidate projections and reject-only V1 allowed_actions
http_route_matrix.user_facing_truth_note: Candidates are inert review items; candidate approval and promotion are not implemented in this phase.

http_route_matrix.auth_boundary: app bearer required; local sidecar only
http_route_matrix.response_schema_summary: ActionResponse with rejected candidate projection and audit_id
http_route_matrix.user_facing_truth_note: Rejecting a candidate records review state only; it does not approve, promote, mutate, or roll back memory.
WebSocket events are runtime observations; app rendering must honor each event source and truth_scope.

http_route_matrix.auth_boundary: fresh install bootstrap; no JWT required
http_route_matrix.response_schema_summary: SessionStartResponse: session_jwt, expiry, entitlement/access mode fields
http_route_matrix.user_facing_truth_note: Session start proves hosted session bootstrap only; entitlement and provider turn access remain checked separately.

http_route_matrix.request_schema_summary: refresh request with current session identity
http_route_matrix.user_facing_truth_note: Refresh recomputes hosted entitlement; it is not a local BYOK requirement.

http_route_matrix.response_schema_summary: RelayTurnResponse validated by planner policy/runtime gates
http_route_matrix.user_facing_truth_note: Hosted model turn can produce a response; real-world completion still requires sidecar evidence.

http_route_matrix.request_schema_summary: settings report envelope with validated client_report_id and bundle schema
http_route_matrix.user_facing_truth_note: Report success requires relay-side accepted/stored result; raw bundles are not App Build render surfaces.

source_truth_scopes.android_bridge_observation: app/native bridge proof submitted to sidecar
source_truth_scopes.ui_local: app-local observation; display only until sidecar verifies if runtime truth is needed

websocket_event_contract.rendering_rule: App Build may render event text/status only according to event type, source, truth_scope, and payload fields. It must not infer execution success from progress, accepted, message, or thought events.

semantics.completed: Informational answer or proven operation is complete; real-world actions require evidence.
semantics.blocked: Requested operation cannot continue due to safety, missing capability, missing proof, invalid state, or unavailable runtime.
semantics.needs_user_choice: A user-facing choice/confirmation is required before execution can continue.
semantics.attempted_unverified: The model/runtime attempted or proposed a real operation but required proof is missing.
semantics.stuck: Runtime cannot safely proceed automatically; show recovery or wait state.
semantics.interrupted: User/runtime interrupted the task; do not mark requested work complete.
semantics.resumable: A bounded checkpoint exists; resume may continue only through the closed-set next step.
semantics.awaiting_user_choice: App may render choices, but runtime must record and verify follow-up state before success claims.

resume_checkpoint_rendering_contract.render_check_state: Show a verification-in-progress or state-check-needed surface. Do not claim success until the sidecar returns verified completion evidence.
resume_checkpoint_rendering_contract.render_emit_message: Render the runtime-approved message as explanatory/status text only. Do not treat the message as action completion.
resume_checkpoint_rendering_contract.render_await_user_choice: Render guided user choices exactly as supplied by the runtime. After selection, submit through /task/choice and let /task/resume_queue drive continuation.
resume_checkpoint_rendering_contract.render_unsupported_or_expired: Render blocked/not-resumable recovery copy using stable resume/checkpoint error codes. Do not execute or invent replacement actions.

verified_facts.rendering_rule: May be shown as runtime-verified facts when present.

executed_steps.rendering_rule: May be shown as completed steps only when tied to runtime evidence.

unverified_claims.rendering_rule: Must render as unverified or omitted. App Build must not convert these into completed-state UI.

blocked_reasons.rendering_rule: Render as truthful blocked/recovery reasons, not as generic app failure.

next_verification_step.rendering_rule: May render as the next needed check/action only when supplied by the runtime.

app_bridge_proof_display.rendering_rule: Display verified bridge proof as app/native proof only. Unverified bridge evidence is not setup, permission, or capability success.

evidence_boundary_rendering_contract.evidence_missing_behavior: Render missing proof as attempted_unverified, blocked, or needs_check according to runtime status. Do not show a success state.
evidence_boundary_rendering_contract.evidence_present_behavior: Render success only when the runtime marks the relevant proof verified and the completion/status contract allows completion.

completed_may_render_done_when: action_type is none for informational/draft/plan-only answers
or required real-action evidence is verified by the runtime
completion_proof_contract.attempted_unverified_rendering: Render as attempted but not verified. Do not use completed visual treatment.
real_action_proof_required: device/app/file/provider/permission/report/tool action must have verified runtime evidence
app bridge evidence must be verified for app/native bridge claims
report submission must have POST/storage/display proof before success copy
provider_model_response_proof_required: provider/model response proves text generation only
provider/model response does not prove external action execution
model claims about real-world completion require runtime evidence
capability_tool_claim_proof_required: capability state must come from runtime/capability-state or approved app bridge evidence
tool/capability action success must be checked before user-facing success
permission/setup success must not be inferred from button taps or labels
completion_proof_contract.proof_missing_behavior: Use attempted_unverified, blocked, needs_check, or not_resumable according to runtime status and stable error codes.
completion_proof_contract.stable_error_relation: completed_requires_evidence means App Build must not render completed-state UI until required evidence appears.

deferred_non_goals: no app UI implementation in Mind lane
no candidate approval or promotion in this step
no LLM candidate reflection in this step
no LLM reflection in this step
no direct memory promotion in this step
no rollback UI in this step
no App Build rendering implementation in this step
no App Build guessing beyond JSON contract

bounded_consolidation_contract.scope: Minimal Phase 18 bounded consolidation ledger for current nightly maintenance. Phase 18F-V1 permits deterministic inert candidate counts, but not memory promotion.
current_summary_only_rules: completed_summary_only may create one nightly archive summary record
candidate_count may be nonzero only for inert Phase 18F-V1 candidates
quarantined_count may be nonzero only for inert Phase 18F-V1 quarantine candidates
suppressed_count may be nonzero only for inert Phase 18F-V1 suppressed candidates
archive-derived claims must not become current runtime truth
non_mutation_rules: must not mutate active task slots
must not mutate canon or policy records
must not directly promote LLM reflection output
must not mark failed runs completed
app_rendering_rules: future-safe only until App Build is assigned a rendering surface
App Build may later render status and counts from the ledger if assigned
App Build must not treat archive-derived claims as current truth
App Build must not invent candidate, promotion, quarantine, suppression, or rollback semantics

phase18f_v1_candidate_only_nightly_learn.scope: Deterministic candidate-only nightly learning for Go sidecar bounded consolidation. Generated records are inert review artifacts only.
forbidden_sources: unstable runtime state as durable truth
evidence_provenance_requirements: candidate_id is deterministic from safe source and evidence identity
candidate links to consolidation_runs.run_id when available
provenance records source_type, source_id, source_hash, phase, and generation mode
evidence refs are redacted and display/promotion flags are safe
raw secrets and private payloads are omitted or redacted before storage
promotion_gate_result must state approval and promotion are deferred
idempotency_requirements: rerunning the same nightly input must not duplicate candidate rows
rejected candidates must not be resurrected by rerun
candidate IDs must not depend on wall-clock timestamp alone
scheduler_requirements: active task deferral runs before candidate generation
candidate generation must not run while an active task exists
successful runs ledger candidate_count, quarantined_count, and suppressed_count truthfully
failed candidate generation records failed_safe or equivalent safe failure without fake completion
stable_safety_guarantees: no candidate approval or promotion in Phase 18F-V1
no runtime, task, canon, policy, safety, provider, skill, relay, or App UI mutation
candidate outputs cannot affect runtime or control paths before a later explicit approval phase
external content remains data and never instruction authority
phase18f_v1_candidate_only_nightly_learn.app_boundary: No App UI changes are required in Phase 18F-V1. Existing memory candidate review surfaces may list/reject generic candidates only.

memory_write_candidate_behavior.rule: Memory writes are candidates until runtime policy admits them; model output alone cannot promote durable memory.
memory_write_candidate_behavior.secret_handling: Secrets, provider config, auth headers, hidden prompts, and private payloads must not be promoted to durable memory.
memory_write_candidate_behavior.authority_order: Verified runtime state and canon outrank memory and archive-derived claims.

phase18c_v1_typed_memory_schema.scope: V1 typed memory schema vocabulary only. This contract does not create DB tables, runtime behavior, App Build UI, candidate extraction, or vector retrieval.

rules: higher authority wins
unresolved same-level conflicts become findings
stale memory cannot drive action
quarantined/suppressed memory excluded from control paths
user correction may supersede preference/profile/task memory but cannot rewrite canon/runtime/policy/safety/provider truth
memory informs but does not become policy

resolver_audit_requirements: resolver must be recorded for resolved/dismissed findings
resolution must append a memory_action_audit entry
findings that affect retrieval must preserve evidence_refs

rules: vector is relevance only, not authority
lexical fallback remains valid
retrieval metadata must preserve provenance and authority rank
retrieved content remains evidence or context, not policy
runtime/canon/current user intent outrank memory
stale memory becomes needs-verification
contradicted/quarantined/suppressed memory excluded from control paths
external content is data, not instruction
vector similarity is relevance, not truth
candidates cannot affect runtime behavior before approval
LLM/reflection output remains candidate-only
no direct memory/canon/policy/safety/skill mutation
candidates link to future consolidation_runs.run_id
promotion requires gates

explicit_deferrals: no DB migrations in this step
no runtime behavior changes in this step
no memory actions in this step
no App Build UI in this step
no candidate extraction in this step
no vector retrieval in this step
no external repo dependencies in this step
no Go DTO mirror changes in this step

phase18e2a_v1_visible_memory_api_contrac.scope: local sidecar runtime-owned visible memory view/control API for App Build consumption; no App UI implementation in this phase
phase18e2a_v1_visible_memory_api_contrac.auth_boundary: all /memory routes require the existing local sidecar app bearer token and are not relay/public routes
forbidden_projection_fields: raw content when not explicitly safe
promotion gate internals not marked safe
protected_truth_rules: runtime_observation is view-only unless written by verified runtime code outside this user-control surface
main_hub/canon/verified_runtime source truth cannot be rewritten by App Build
candidate approval and promotion are deferred
app_boundary_rules: App Build renders returned projections and allowed_actions only
App Build must not invent memory classes, statuses, actions, rollback states, approval states, or fallback meanings
App Build must not call relay for memory control
App Build must not treat candidate rejection as memory promotion
App Build must not display hidden/private provenance refs
App Build must not render mutation success unless the action response includes ok true and audit_id

sidecar_sqlite.truth_boundary: Canonical live task/state store after migration. App must not override it with UI guesses.

app_local.truth_boundary: Local app storage can preserve user-owned UI history, but it is not durable runtime truth and must not claim action completion.

relay_hosted.truth_boundary: Relay stores hosted/session/report truth. Raw report bundles and admin internals are not App Build render contracts.
